text: Managing a team requires a careful balance of several key skills, with communication being the most important. A good team leader ensures everyone understands their roles and the project's goals through clear, open dialogue alongside delegation—assigning tasks based on each member's strengths to avoid overworking anyone. Giving timely feedback, both positive and constructive, helps team members improve and feel valued, which naturally boosts motivation. When conflicts arise, as they often do, effective conflict resolution means listening to all sides and finding a fair compromise. By combining these practices, a leader can create a supportive and productive environment where everyone works well together.
